• Smart Billing
    Smart Electric E-bill
    • This presentation delves into the analysis of IoT-based Smart Electric Billing Systems, highlighting their technological advancements, significance, and impact on energy management.
    Register Now
    Swarith Reddy -- 23911A05J5 Neela Sai -- 23911A05H1 Manikanta -- 23911A05J7 Shima Tej -- 23911A05K0
    Presenter
    Guide : P.Lakshmi Priya
  • Abstract
    An innovative IoT-based system designed to automate electric billing, providing accurate readings and enhancing energy management efficiency.
    Smart Energy Meter
    The automatic electricity billing system utilizes smart meters and advanced communication technologies to capture and transmit real-time electricity consumption data to utility providers. This automation ensures precise billing and reduces manual errors, while consumers benefit from detailed insights into their energy usage for better management and cost savings. Real-time data enables utility companies to monitor energy distribution, detect outages, and implement effective demand response strategies. The integration of IoT technology provides features like remote disconnection and reconnection, real-time outage detection, and automated demand-side management, enhancing the system's resilience and reliability. This fosters a more transparent, efficient, and sustainable energy framework, benefiting both consumers and utility providers by improving operational efficiency and promoting sustainable energy consumption.

  • Research work
    • Literature Survey on IoT Research Using Scholarly Papers, YouTube, Google, and SI Resources

    • Current smart meters often incur high costs and lack remote updates.

    • Human errors can result in incorrect bill estimations, causing consumer dissatisfaction.

    • Manual meter reading is slow, prone to errors, and inefficient in India.

    Proposed System
    Automated Electricity Meter Reading and Billing System  An Overview of the IoT-Based Solution
  • 1
    Smart Meter
    • Incorporates ESP8266, Arduino, or Raspberry Pi to capture energy readings efficiently.
    Connector text
    2
    Data Transmission
    • Wi-Fi technology transmits meter readings to a cloud-based billing system seamlessly.
    Connector text
    3
    User Dashboard
    • Consumers can easily monitor their energy usage through web or mobile applications.
    Connector text
    4
    Automated Billing
    • Bills are generated based on real-time data, with instant notifications sent to users.
    Connector text
    5
    Eliminates Human Error
    • Reduces the potential for errors and manual effort in energy billing processes.
    Connector text
    6
    Reduces Billing Disputes
    • Minimizes conflicts over billing amounts and addresses power theft effectively.
    Connector text
    7
    Real-time Tracking
    • Empowers consumers to track their energy usage and predict costs in real-time.
    Connector text
    8
    Cost-Effective Solution
    • Offers a more affordable alternative compared to traditional Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) setups.
    Connector text
    Smart Meter System Architecture and Advantages
    Understanding the architecture and benefits of smart meters
  • 1
    1. Data Acquisition Module (ESP32)
    • Reads meter values and transmits data via Wi-Fi for real-time access.
    Connector text
    2
    2. Cloud-Based Processing Module
    • Stores and processes meter readings securely for efficient billing.
    Connector text
    3
    3. User Interface Module
    • Offers a web or mobile dashboard for comprehensive, real-time monitoring.
    Connector text
    4
    4. Automated Alert System
    • Sends notifications via SMS or email when threshold limits are surpassed.
    Connector text
    5
    5. Security & Authentication Module
    • Employs encryption methods to prevent unauthorized data access and tampering.
    System Modules Overview
    Exploring the Key Components of the System
  • Innovative Solutions for Energy Management
    • Addressing Research Gaps in Energy Solutions
    Call To Action
    1
    Real-Time Demand-Based Billing
    • Existing systems lack dynamic rate adjustments based on usage patterns.
    Call To Action
    2
    Adaptive Pricing Models
    • Proposed system introduces flexible pricing based on peak and off-peak demands.
    Call To Action
    3
    Offline Mode for IoT Meters
    • Current IoT meters depend on constant connectivity, limiting their effectiveness.
    Call To Action
    4
    Energy Theft Detection
    • Hybrid models need local storage and anomaly detection to prevent unauthorized consumption.
    Call To Action
    5
    Integration with Renewable Energy
    • Smart meters often do not track solar energy; integration can enhance monitoring.
    Call To Action
    6
    Solar Net Metering
    • Adding solar net metering can optimize tracking of solar versus grid electricity.
    Call To Action
  • Transformative Energy Management
    • The IoT-based system revolutionizes energy management, enhancing efficiency over traditional methods.

The automatic electricity billing system utilizes smart meters and advanced communication technologies to capture and transmit real-time electricity consumption data to utility providers. This automation ensures precise billing and reduces manual errors, while consumers benefit from detailed insights into their energy usage for better management and cost savings. Real-time data enables utility companies to monitor energy distribution, detect outages, and implement effective demand response strategies. The integration of IoT technology provides features like remote disconnection and reconnection, real-time outage detection, and automated demand-side management, enhancing the system's resilience and reliability.
